# Contributing to LAMMPS-GUI via GitHub

Thank you for considering to contribute to the LAMMPS-GUI software.

The following is a set of guidelines as well as explanations of policies
and work flows for contributing to the LAMMPS molecular dynamics
software project. These guidelines focus on submitting issues or pull
requests on the LAMMPS-GUI GitHub project.

## Table of Contents

[I don't want to read this whole thing, I just have a question!](#i-dont-want-to-read-this-whole-thing-i-just-have-a-question)

[How Can I Contribute?](#how-can-i-contribute)

[GitHub Work flows](#github-workflows)

## I don't want to read this whole thing I just have a question!

> **Note:** Please do not file an issue to ask a general question about
> LAMMPS-GUI or LAMMPS, its features, how to use specific commands, or
> how perform simulations or analysis in LAMMPS. Instead post your
> question to the [LAMMPS Material Science Discourse
> forum](https://matsci.org/lammps).  Before posting to the forum,
> please read the forum specific [guidelines and
> suggestions](https://matsci.org/t/please-read-this-first-guidelines-and-suggestions-for-posting-lammps-questions/49913).
> Following those guidelines and suggestions will help greatly to get a
> helpful response.  *Always* mention which LAMMPS-GUI and LAMMPS
> version you are using. The MatSci website may be also used for
> discussions that would be off-topic for the LAMMPS categories.  Those
> will just have to be posted to a different category.

## How Can I Contribute?

### Reporting Bugs

While code for LAMMPS-GUI is carefully tested, the complexity of
graphical applications and how users interact with it, and making
certain that it behaves consistently across multiple platforms, it is
impossible to test for all possible use cases and activities.  Thus bugs
do happen, and if you suspect, that you have encountered one, please try
to document it and report it as an
[Issue](https://github.com/akohlmey/lammps-gui/issues) on the LAMMPS-GUI
GitHub project web page.

To be able to submit an issue on GitHub, you have to register for an
account (for GitHub in general).  If you do not want to do that, or have
other reservations against submitting an issue there, you can - as an
alternative and in decreasing preference - either send an e-mail to the
original author of LAMMPS-GUI, akohlmey@gmail.com.

### Suggesting Enhancements

Suggestions for enhancements or new features are always welcome.  These
should be submitted using the [GitHub Issue
Tracker](https://github.com/akohlmey/lammps-gui/issues) of the
LAMMPS-GUI project.  This is particularly recommended, when you plan to
implement the feature or enhancement yourself, as this allows to
coordinate in case there are other similar or conflicting ongoing
developments.

Your submission will be reviewed and considered for implementation.
Whether this will actually happen depends on many factors: how difficult
it would be, how much effort it would take, how many users would benefit
from it, and whether this is a feature that would fit into the scope of
a software like LAMMPS-GUI.  Because of these factors, it matters how
well the suggested enhancement is formulated and the overall benefit is
argued convincingly.

To be able to submit an issue on GitHub, you have to register for an
account (for GitHub in general).

### Contributing Code

Contributed code is highly welcome, however it has to fit into the
concept for LAMMPS-GUI and has to be highly portable and consistent
across the supported platforms.  The included documentation lists what
platforms and library versions, especially Qt, LAMMPS-GUI is compatible
with.
